skill.name: seedance-v2
skill.description: Use ByteDance Seedance 2.0 Pro through RunComfy to generate short cinematic videos with text, image, video, and audio references. The skill documents the model schema, prompting guidance, CLI commands, output handling, and routing advice for related video models.
skill.summary: Generate short cinematic Seedance 2.0 Pro videos through the RunComfy CLI.
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many command, network, filesystem, and blocker patterns, but most high-severity blocker hits are Markdown false positives. The confirmed risks are legitimate for this skill: it instructs agents to run the RunComfy CLI, submit prompts and media references to an external model API, download generated files, and rely on local token storage. No evidence found of malicious intent or prompt injection text in SKILL.md.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Execution Required
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill intentionally directs the agent to install, log in to, and run the RunComfy CLI. This is a real execution risk because commands run locally and process user-provided prompt input, even though the documented command uses JSON input and no direct shell expansion was found.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command behavior is explicitly documented in SKILL.md and is central to the skill. Confidence is below very high because the file states prompt content is passed as JSON, which lowers command injection likelihood.

medium_findings.title: External Network Submission and Downloads
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill sends prompts and media references to the RunComfy Model API and downloads generated output URLs. This can expose prompt text, supplied media URLs, and generated files to a third-party service, which requires user awareness and trust in the provider.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network use is clearly described with an API endpoint and output download domains. The domains appear related to the declared service, so this is a privacy and supply-chain risk rather than evidence of exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Local Token and Generated File Handling
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill documents local token storage and generated file downloads into a selected output directory. This is a filesystem risk because credentials and large generated files are handled locally, although the file claims token permissions are owner-only and downloads have a size cap.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem behavior is directly documented. The security impact is moderate because the described paths and token handling are expected for a CLI integration, not hidden behavior.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Static Hits Are Placeholder URLs
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The path traversal findings map to placeholder media URLs using ellipses in examples, not filesystem traversal code. No evidence found that the skill constructs paths from untrusted input or uses ../ sequences to access local files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is inside Markdown examples for remote image, video, and audio references. The surrounding context describes API media inputs, not local path access.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Hits Are Text False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The weak cryptography findings align with ordinary prose, headings, or model guidance. No evidence found of cryptographic code, hashing functions, cipher selection, or security-sensitive algorithm use in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill is Markdown documentation and contains no implementation code for encryption or hashing. The static pattern appears to match incidental words rather than executable crypto logic.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Static Hits Are Routing and Specification Text
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The system reconnaissance findings occur in prose about model routing and media requirements. No evidence found of commands that inspect the host, enumerate files, collect environment data, or fingerprint the system.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant lines are plain user guidance, not executable host inspection. The broader file does not contain reconnaissance commands.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Examples for RunComfy
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill contains shell command examples for npx, npm, runcomfy login, and runcomfy run. These commands are expected for this integration but require user approval and careful argument handling.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible in Markdown blocks and prerequisites. They are not hidden, but they would execute locally if followed.

dangerous_patterns.title: Third-Party API Endpoint
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents POST requests to RunComfy model infrastructure and output downloads from RunComfy domains. This is expected behavior but should be disclosed as third-party processing.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The endpoint and domains are explicit. They match the skill purpose, so the risk is transparency and data handling rather than confirmed abuse.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential Storage Path Mentioned
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill states that RunComfy login writes an API token under the user config directory. Credential files must be protected and should not be shared through prompts, logs, or generated artifacts.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The token path is directly stated. The line also claims restrictive permissions, which reduces but does not remove credential-handling risk.

content.value_statement: Short video generation often needs consistent characters, media references, and clear model settings. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Seedance 2.0 Pro prompts and RunComfy CLI invocation.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to choose Seedance 2.0 Pro instead of related video models.
Documents supported prompt, image, video, audio, aspect ratio, duration, resolution, audio, and seed inputs.
Provides RunComfy CLI examples for text-only, image-reference, and multimodal video generation.
Gives prompting guidance for camera motion, audio tone, stable identity, and reference media use.
Documents RunComfy exit codes, output downloads, token handling, and external endpoints.
limitations: Requires the RunComfy CLI, a RunComfy account, and valid authentication.
Video duration is limited to 4 to 15 seconds on the documented endpoint.
Resolution is limited to 480p or 720p in the documented schema.
Reference media URLs and prompts are processed by the RunComfy service.

use_cases.title: Create Lip-Synced Product Ads
use_cases.description: Generate short spokesperson clips using a character image, concise script direction, and vertical social video settings.

use_cases.title: Prototype Cinematic Scenes
use_cases.description: Turn camera, lighting, movement, and reference media notes into short previs clips for review.

use_cases.title: Build Brand-Consistent Variants
use_cases.description: Use fixed identity references and seeds to create short video variants across messages or languages.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Text Video
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple short clip.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 5 second Seedance 2.0 Pro video from this idea: [describe scene]. Use 720p, adaptive aspect ratio, and generated audio.

prompt_templates.title: Image Reference Spokesperson
prompt_templates.scenario: Use a person or product image to hold identity.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Seedance 2.0 Pro prompt and RunComfy command for a 9:16 spokesperson clip. Use this image URL as the identity reference: [image URL]. The person should say: [short script].

prompt_templates.title: Multimodal Scene Match
prompt_templates.scenario: Combine image, video, and audio references.
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a Seedance 2.0 Pro multimodal generation using this image, video, and audio reference set: [URLs]. Match the subject, scene motion, and voice tone while keeping the output under 15 seconds.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Variant Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Plan reproducible video variants for testing.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design three Seedance 2.0 Pro variants for this campaign: [campaign brief]. Specify duration, aspect ratio, reference media roles, seed strategy, and why each variant differs.

output_examples.input: A social ad needs a friendly cafe spokesperson using one headshot and a short product message.
output: Recommended Seedance 2.0 Pro because image identity and native lip sync are important.
Suggested a vertical 8 second clip with a medium close-up, slow push-in, warm window light, and generated cafe ambience.
Identified the headshot as the image reference and the spoken message as prompt text.

output_examples.input: A creative team has a location video, a subject image, and a voice tone reference.
output: Mapped the subject image to identity, the location video to scene motion, and the audio file to tone guidance.
Recommended keeping all reference media within the documented 2 to 15 second limits.
Produced a concise generation plan with aspect ratio, duration, and prompt direction.

output_examples.input: A user asks whether Seedance or another RunComfy video model fits an edit request.
output: Explained that Seedance fits new short-form multimodal generation with lip sync.
Suggested alternate models when the request centers on motion editing, audio-driven lip sync, or ultra-fast iteration.

best_practices: Put stable identity, costumes, products, and logos in image references instead of long verbal descriptions.
Use plain camera and motion language, such as medium close-up, slow push-in, or locked-off wide shot.
Confirm that reference videos and audio meet duration, size, and format limits before running the CLI.
anti_patterns: Do not pass private media URLs unless the user accepts third-party processing by RunComfy.
Do not ask for clips longer than 15 seconds on this documented endpoint.
Do not mix conflicting visual styles or contradictory motion instructions in one prompt.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It helps an AI assistant prepare and run Seedance 2.0 Pro video generations through RunComfy.

faq.question: Does it require a RunComfy account?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented workflow requires the RunComfy CLI and authentication through login or a token.

faq.question: Can it use image, video, and audio references?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented schema supports up to 9 images, 3 videos, and 3 audio references.

faq.question: What video length can it generate?
faq.answer: The documented endpoint supports whole-second durations from 4 to 15 seconds.

faq.question: Where are generated files saved?
faq.answer: The RunComfy CLI downloads generated output URLs into the output directory chosen in the command.

faq.question: What are the main security considerations?
faq.answer: The skill runs a local CLI, uses external network services, handles media URLs, and relies on RunComfy token storage.
